name: Chocolate Truffle Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Strong brewed coffee or espresso
6 oz Bittersweet chocolate, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
2 oz Unsweetened chocolate, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter a 9 or 10 inch springform pan and dust with cocoa, tapping out excess. In a 2-quart glass bowl, combine butter, sugar, coffee, and both chocolates. Microwave on high for 2 minutes, or until the mixture is smooth when stirred. Whisk in eggs until well blended. Pour mixture into springform pan. Bake 50-55 minutes, or until the top of the cake forms a crust and feels dry to the touch. Let cool in the pan.
1. Run a knife around the edge of the pan to loosen the cake.
2. Remove the side of the pan.
3. Serve warm or refrigerated, in small slices. Store covered cake in the refrigerator. Serve alone or with whipped cream, vanilla ice cream, or make it really elegant and serve in a pool of raspberry sauce. This is dense and rich and oh so good...words just don't do it justice. Best of all, it's super easy to make - only 10 minutes and one bowl to mix everything, and you don't even need the mixer out. The original recipe comes from "365 Great Chocolate Desserts" by Natalie Haughton, who calls it "Chocolate Truffle Cake."
